NavDescription:
Evan is a doctoral student in the Counseling Psychology doctoral program at UAlbany.  

Evan previously worked for six years as an addiction recovery coach supporting individuals at all stages of recovery. After completing his master’s degree in mental health counseling, he worked as a recovery support navigator at the Center for Behavioral Health Promotion and Applied Research. 

He is currently the Lead Prevention Navigator for Project ACCESS, a prevention service grant aimed at helping minoritized students navigate health and well-being resources on- and off-campus.  

Evan’s research interests broadly focus on addiction and positive psychology. 

In his free time, you can find Evan hiking in the Catskills, snowboarding in Vermont or practicing meditation. 

Pronouns: He/him/his
VirtualInfo: Virtual Appointments are on WEDNESDAYS ONLY (Video on is required)

NavDescription:
Clare is a doctoral student in the Counseling Psychology program at UAlbany. She received her master’s degree in Psychology in Education (M.A.) from Teachers College, Columbia University with a dual focus in global mental health & trauma, and sexuality, women, & gender. She earned her bachelor’s degree in theater with a minor in religion from Vassar College.
 
Clare’s research interests include suicide prevention and crisis intervention for high-risk populations. She is particularly interested in making care timely, inclusive, and relevant.
Prior to returning to school, Clare had a career in documentaries, and many of the films she worked on appeared on PBS. Clare is an avid rower, skater, and hiker.

Pronouns: She/Her/Hers
VirtualInfo: Virtual Appointments are on WEDNESDAYS & FRIDAYS ONLY (Video on is required)

NavDescription:
Meg is a doctoral student in the Counseling Psychology program at UAlbany and has worked as a Prevention Navigator on Project ACCESS since the Fall of 2022. Meg is passionate about promoting harm reduction on UAlbany’s campus and providing students with a safe space to discuss their well-being. In her free time, Meg enjoys reading, rock climbing, photography, and spending time with friends and family.
Pronouns: She/her/hers
VirtualInfo: Virtual Appointments are on FRIDAYS ONLY (Video on is required)

NavDescription:
Ashleigh is a doctoral student in the Clinical Psychology doctoral program at UAlbany and recently joined Project ACCESS as a Prevention Navigator. After graduating from the University of Florida with a bachelor's degree in Psychology, she worked as a clinical research coordinator in the Heifets Lab at Stanford University examining novel psychoactive interventions for mental health. She currently conducts practice oriented research as a member of the Boswell lab, and her interests include implementation science, dissemination of evidence-based practices, and clinical decision-making. Outside of research, Ashleigh loves to sing in choir, read mystery novels, bake, and travel! 

Pronouns: She/her/hers
VirtualInfo: Virtual Appointments are on WEDNESDAYS ONLY (Video on is required)

NavDescription: Alex M. Ray is a Ph.D. student in the counseling psychology program at UAlbany and recently joined Project ACCESS as a Prevention Navigator. Alex's research interests broadly focus on substance misuse, psychological assessment, and psychometrics. In his free time, he enjoys reading and collecting spider-man comic books, watching movies, and playing guitar. Pronouns: he/him/his

Genesis is an MSW/MPH student at UAlbany focusing on Health Policy & Management and Social Welfare, while earning a certification in Maternal and Child Health. Driven by her passion for advocacy and social justice, she recently served with the Empire State Summer Corps to address food insecurity in Scotia, NY. 
Genesis currently holds a B.A. in Psychology from Marymount Manhattan College and an A.A. in Psychology from Borough of Manhattan Community College. As a researcher, she has been a recipient of the Richard H. Wexler Award for her work on Industrial-Organizational psychology at St. Francis College. Her paper, “Facing Reality: The Psychology Behind Death and Dying,” is featured in Nava Silton’s Challenges and Opportunities for Women, Parenting, and Child Development. Outside of her academic and advocacy work, you will find Genesis playing video games on her playstation 5, going to concerts, traveling across states, or journaling. 
Pronouns: She/Her/Hers"

Ayanfe Adebajo is a Master of Public Health student in Epidemiology at the University at Albany. She earned her bachelor’s degree in Public Health with a minor in Psychology from SUNY Brockport. Her interests include health equity, maternal and reproductive health, mental health, and the social determinants of health. She also owns her own hair business and creates digital content, blending creativity with community engagement.
Pronouns: She/Her/Hers
